description: Heraclitus was almost certainly familiar with Anaximander's work; both were Ionian thinkers working on similar questions within a generation of each other. Anaximander's emphasis on the tension between opposites — and his idea that the world is sustained by their conflict — is a clear precursor to Heraclitus's doctrine of the unity of opposites and the role of strife in maintaining cosmic order. Heraclitus sharpens Anaximander's cosmological intuition into a fully developed theory of flux and Logos.
source: Charles Kahn, The Art and Thought of Heraclitus (1979)
